# Transcrição: Lesson 20 - How to Stop Comparing Yourself to Others

- **Fonte:** Lesson 20 - How to Stop Comparing Yourself to Others
- **Duração:** 01:58
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:58]

So, one of the things that comes out of discussions about behavioral genetics and what we were born with or what we weren't born with is, well, what if I want to do something that I'm not designed for? What if I want to do something that I just don't happen to have, you know, great ability at? What if I'm five foot six and I want to play basketball or, you know, something like that? Well, to that I would say, first of all, you're totally welcome to try, you know, anything that you'd like. There's no one that says you can't do something or can't try something simply because you weren't designed to be good at it. And there are plenty of examples of people who have overcome the odds. But I think the key lesson here is that any one of us could be a billion things. And there are often many different ways to get to success, right? To define that, to achieve the things that we're looking for. And very rarely is there just one path to achieving that success. There are probably many different lives that you could live all with equal levels of happiness or, you know, joy and success. And so the first is to realize that. The second is to shift from what I would call a between person mindset and shift to an within person mindset. So often discussions about behavioral genetics or what we are or aren't capable of are all between person. There are comparisons about, you know, what you can do compared to what someone else could do. And instead, if we can shift internally and think about what we're capable of now and how we can fashion our skills, improve ourselves and otherwise optimize the hidden forces that surround us to shape our habits and behaviors and improve our standing, then it becomes a much more interesting discussion because it's less about how you measure up to those around you and more about how you improve compared to the previous self that you were. And that I think is the focus that we should take as we transition into the next hidden force that shapes our habits and behavior, which is mindset. So keep that in mind as we move forward and I'll see you in the next lesson.
